Code fragment: public final TableField<FileRecord, UInteger> FID = createField(DSL.name("fid"), org.jooq.impl.SQLDataType.INTEGERUNSIGNED.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The table has a synthetic auto-increment primary key 'fid' even though a natural unique key already exists on (owner_uid, name) via KEY_FILE_OWNER_UID. Consider using the composite key (owner_uid, name) as the primary key since file names are unique per owner and these columns are relatively stable.

Code fragment: public final TableField<ProjectUserAccessRecord, ProjectUserAccessPrivilege> PRIVILEGE = createField(DSL.name("privilege"), org.jooq.impl.SQLDataType.VARCHAR(5).nullable(false).defaultValue(org.jooq.impl.DSL.inline("NONE", org.jooq.impl.SQLDataType.VARCHAR)).asEnumDataType(edu.uci.ics.texera.web.model.jooq.generated.enums.ProjectUserAccessPrivilege.class), this, "");

Code fragment: public final TableField<UserRecord, UInteger> UID = createField(DSL.name("uid"), org.jooq.impl.SQLDataType.INTEGERUNSIGNED.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The table has a synthetic auto-increment primary key 'uid' even though there's already a unique constraint on 'name' (KEY_USER_NAME) which could serve as a natural primary key. Using WORKFLOW.fields() selects all columns from the WORKFLOW table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and cause maintenance issues if the table schema changes. Consider explicitly listing only the required columns in the select clause.
